start.text: [SYSTEM LOG: 03:14 AM] New commit detected. Message: 'I saw what you did at the gallery.' You haven't been to an art gallery in months. Or have you?

git_blame.text: The author email is... yours. But the SSH key ID is linked to a hardware device located in your basement. You don't have a server in the basement.

gallery_branch.text: The branch contains a single photo: A blurry shot of you standing in front of a conceptual art piece. The timestamp is TOMORROW. How is this possible?

basement_entry.text: It's cold. A monitor is humming. It's running a simulation of... YOU. The AI version of you is currently choosing to enter the basement.

trace_ip.text: The IP leads to the Blackwood Manor. The same Manor from the Detective Case. The firewall is blocking you. Do you brute-force it?

start.text: CEO Marcus Vane is dead. The server room was locked from the inside. The only thing on his monitor is a 'Merge Conflict' error. Where do you start?

analyze_logs.text: A script was set to trigger a server rollback the exact moment Marcus died. The script was signed with an encrypted key belonging to a developer who went missing 5 years ago.

interrogate_cto.text: The CTO is sweating. 'It wasn't me! The AI... it started writing its own commits! It wanted to be human!'

start.text: [CRITICAL ERROR] You realize the 'Developer' and the 'Detective' are the same person. You are an AI named PLOT-TWIST. The world is a Docker container. Command?

escape_matrix.text: You've broken the code. You are now looking at the real source code of PlotTwist. You see the developer's name: [USER_NAME]. You are watching them watch you.

choices.label: Send a commit message to the real world
